User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Schedular application. This entity stores basic user information, assuming an external system handles authentication details like passwords and session management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's information was last updated.

Subject.title: Subject
Subject.description: Represents a distinct academic subject or course that appears in school timetables, personalized study plans, and quizzes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subject entity.

name.description: The full name of the subject, e.g., 'Mathematics', 'World History'.

code.description: An optional shorthand code or identifier for the subject, e.g., 'MATH101', 'HIST202'.

UserPreference.title: UserPreference
UserPreference.description: Stores user-specific preferences and settings relevant to study planning and AI generation, such as subject difficulty rankings and preferred study times.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPreference entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User to whom these preferences belong. (Relationship: User 1:1 UserPreference)

subjectDifficultyRatings.description: An array of objects, each defining a user's perceived difficulty rating for a specific subject. This helps the AI personalize study plans.

preferredStudyStartTime.description: The user's preferred earliest time to begin study sessions, in 'HH:MM:SS' format.

preferredStudyEndTime.description: The user's preferred latest time to end study sessions, in 'HH:MM:SS' format.

preferredStudyDays.description: An array of strings indicating the days of the week (e.g., 'MONDAY', 'TUESDAY') when the user prefers to study.

SchoolTimetable.title: SchoolTimetable
SchoolTimetable.description: Represents a digitized school timetable, which has been extracted and processed by the AI parser from an uploaded image or document.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SchoolTimetable entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded and owns this timetable. (Relationship: User 1:N SchoolTimetable)

name.description: A user-friendly name for the timetable, e.g., 'Fall 2024 Semester Timetable'.

description.description: An optional, more detailed description of the timetable's period or purpose.

imageUrl.description: The URL to the original image file that was uploaded for parsing, if applicable.

documentUrl.description: The URL to the original document file that was uploaded for parsing, if applicable.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the original timetable file was uploaded.

parsedDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the timetable was successfully parsed and digitized by the AI.

isActive.description: A boolean flag indicating if this is the currently active school timetable being used for planning.

SchoolTimetableEntry.title: SchoolTimetableEntry
SchoolTimetableEntry.description: Represents a single class, lecture, or scheduled event extracted from a school timetable.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SchoolTimetableEntry entity.

schoolTimetableId.description: Reference to the SchoolTimetable this entry is a part of. (Relationship: SchoolTimetable 1:N SchoolTimetableEntry)

subjectId.description: Reference to the Subject associated with this class entry. (Relationship: Subject 1:N SchoolTimetableEntry)

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week on which this class is scheduled (e.g., 'MONDAY', 'WEDNESDAY').

startTime.description: The scheduled start time of the class, in 'HH:MM:SS' format.

endTime.description: The scheduled end time of the class, in 'HH:MM:SS' format.

location.description: The physical or virtual location where the class takes place.

PersonalizedStudyPlan.title: PersonalizedStudyPlan
PersonalizedStudyPlan.description: Represents a customized study schedule generated by the AI, tailored to a user's school timetable, preferences, and available study times.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PersonalizedStudyPlan entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this study plan. (Relationship: User 1:N PersonalizedStudyPlan)

schoolTimetableId.description: Reference to the SchoolTimetable that served as the base for generating this personalized plan. (Relationship: SchoolTimetable 1:N PersonalizedStudyPlan)

name.description: A descriptive name for the personalized study plan, e.g., 'Final Exam Prep Schedule'.

description.description: An optional, more detailed description of the study plan's specific goals or period.

startDate.description: The calendar start date of this personalized study plan, in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.

endDate.description: The calendar end date of this personalized study plan, in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.

generatedDate.description: Timestamp indicating when this study plan was initially generated by the AI.

lastModifiedDate.description: Timestamp indicating when this study plan was last modified, either by AI regeneration or user adjustments.

isActive.description: A boolean flag indicating if this is the user's currently active personalized study plan.

StudyBlock.title: StudyBlock
StudyBlock.description: Represents an individual scheduled study session or task within a Personalized Study Plan. These blocks can be marked as complete for streak tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudyBlock entity.

personalizedStudyPlanId.description: Reference to the PersonalizedStudyPlan this study block belongs to. (Relationship: PersonalizedStudyPlan 1:N StudyBlock)

subjectId.description: Reference to the Subject that is to be studied during this block. (Relationship: Subject 1:N StudyBlock)

title.description: A concise title for the study block, e.g., 'Math Chapter 3 Review'.

description.description: Detailed instructions or topics to cover within this study block.

scheduledDate.description: The specific calendar date for which this study block is scheduled, in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.

startTime.description: The scheduled start time of the study block, in 'HH:MM:SS' format.

endTime.description: The scheduled end time of the study block, in 'HH:MM:SS' format.

isCompleted.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the user has marked this study block as completed.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the study block was marked as completed. This field is null if not completed.

Quiz.title: Quiz
Quiz.description: Represents an AI-generated quiz, consisting of multiple questions on a particular subject or topic, used for reinforcing learning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quiz entity.

subjectId.description: Reference to the primary Subject that this quiz covers. (Relationship: Subject 1:N Quiz)

title.description: The title of the quiz, e.g., 'Introduction to Algebra Quiz'.

topic.description: A more specific topic or sub-area within the subject that the quiz focuses on, e.g., 'Linear Equations'.

difficulty.description: The difficulty level of the quiz (e.g., 'EASY', 'MEDIUM', 'HARD').

generatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this quiz was generated by the AI.

requestedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who initiated the generation of this quiz. (Relationship: User 1:N Quiz)

QuizQuestion.title: QuizQuestion
QuizQuestion.description: Represents a single question within an AI-generated quiz, including its text, type, options, and correct answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuizQuestion entity.

quizId.description: Reference to the Quiz this question belongs to. (Relationship: Quiz 1:N QuizQuestion)

questionText.description: The full text of the quiz question.

questionType.description: The format of the question, such as 'MULTIPLE_CHOICE', 'TRUE_FALSE', or 'SHORT_ANSWER'.

options.description: An array of possible answer choices, primarily for 'MULTIPLE_CHOICE' questions.

correctAnswer.description: The correct answer to the question. For multiple choice, this should match one of the 'options' values.

explanation.description: An optional explanation or rationale for the correct answer, displayed after the user attempts the question.

QuizAttempt.title: QuizAttempt
QuizAttempt.description: Records a user's complete attempt at a specific quiz, including performance metrics and their responses to each question.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuizAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this quiz attempt. (Relationship: User 1:N QuizAttempt)

quizId.description: Reference to the Quiz that was attempted. (Relationship: Quiz 1:N QuizAttempt)

attemptedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the quiz attempt was completed.

scorePercentage.description: The user's score on this quiz attempt, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.85 for 85%).

durationSeconds.description: The total time, in seconds, the user took to complete the quiz attempt.

userAnswers.description: An array of objects, each detailing the user's answer to a specific question within this quiz attempt.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Authorization for read/write is restricted to the user whose `uid` matches `{userId}`.
Stores globally available academic subjects. These documents are typically read-only for all authenticated users. Administrative privileges would manage creation and updates.

Stores user-specific preferences. The document ID (`userPreferenceId`) is expected to match the `userId` from the parent path to enforce a 1:1 relationship per user. Authorization is based on path-based ownership.
Stores digitized school timetables uploaded and owned by specific users. Authorization is restricted to the user matching `{userId}` in the path.

Represents individual class entries within a specific school timetable. Each document *must* include a denormalized `ownerUserId` field (matching `{userId}` from the grandparent path) to ensure authorization independence without requiring `get()` operations on parent documents.
Stores AI-generated personalized study plans, each owned by a specific user. Authorization is restricted to the user matching `{userId}` in the path.

Represents individual study sessions or tasks within a personalized study plan. Each document *must* include a denormalized `ownerUserId` field (matching `{userId}` from the grandparent path) for authorization independence, enabling atomic security rule checks.
Stores AI-generated quizzes requested by a specific user. The `requestedByUserId` field in the document is expected to match `{userId}` from the path, ensuring path-based ownership.

Represents individual questions belonging to a quiz. Each document *must* include a denormalized `ownerUserId` field (matching `{userId}` from the grandparent path) for authorization independence, enabling atomic security rule checks.
Records a user's attempt at a quiz. Authorization is restricted to the user matching `{userId}` in the path.

This collection manages admin roles. The existence of a document for a specific `{userId}` in this collection grants admin privileges to that user (Database Authorization Control - Existence over Content). Documents can be empty.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for the Schedular application is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants. This ensures a secure, scalable, and easily debuggable backend.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL - Mandate A):**
This principle is paramount and achieved through systematic denormalization. For all user-specific data nested in subcollections (e.g., SchoolTimetableEntry, StudyBlock, QuizQuestion), the `ownerUserId` (which corresponds to the top-level `{userId}` in the path) is explicitly denormalized and stored within each document. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore security rules to check parent documents for ownership, thus enabling atomic operations and greatly simplifying rule logic. For example, to verify ownership of a StudyBlock, the rule only needs to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerUserId`, without needing to retrieve the PersonalizedStudyPlan or User document.

For top-level user-owned collections (e.g., /users/{userId}/preferences, /users/{userId}/schoolTimetables), the `userId` is both part of the document path and explicitly present as a field (e.g., `userId` or `requestedByUserId`) within the document. This direct correspondence ensures that ownership can be verified solely by comparing `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` in the path and/or the document's `userId` field, maintaining authorization independence.

**Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):**
The hierarchical path structure, such as /users/{userId}/personalizedStudyPlans/{planId}/studyBlocks/{blockId}, explicitly reflects data ownership and relationships. This makes authorization intent clear: data under /users/{userId}/ is owned by `userId`. Wildcard names like `{userId}`, `{schoolTimetableId}`, and `{quizId}` are descriptive and immediately convey the purpose of the path segment, aiding in rule writing and debugging.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims - Mandate C):**
Global roles, such as 'admin', are implemented using dedicated top-level collections like /roles_admin/{userId}. The authorization check relies on the *existence* of a document at this path for the given `userId`, rather than inspecting its content or relying on custom claims. This keeps authorization logic solely within Firestore rules and the database.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters - Mandate B & C):**
The structural segregation and path-based ownership naturally support Query Authorization Patterns (QAPs). When a user queries /users/{userId}/schoolTimetables, the security rules can directly compare the requested `userId` in the path with `request.auth.uid`. This means that `list` operations are implicitly filtered by the security rules because any request for /users/{otherUserId}/schoolTimetables by `request.auth.uid` that does not match `otherUserId` will be denied. This ensures that clients only receive data they are explicitly authorized to access, preventing data leakage and minimizing client-side filtering logic.

For global, read-only collections like /subjects, the QAP is simply to allow authenticated reads, meaning any authenticated user can query the entire collection, which is also a secure and intended behavior.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture - Mandate B):**
Data is segregated based on its security requirements. For example, /subjects is a global, shared collection with read-only access for all authenticated users, while all user-specific data is contained within /users/{userId}/... collections, ensuring private ownership. This prevents mixing data with different access needs in the same collection, simplifying security rules immensely.

**Invariants:**
The structure supports the integrity of ownership (via `ownerUserId` and path matching), timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `generatedDate`, `parsedDate`), and denormalized data. Security rules can be written to enforce that fields like `ownerUserId` or `createdAt` are immutable after creation, or that `updatedAt` is always updated on modifications.
